mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer spending, which drives corporate revenue and earnings. This fundamental demand strength supports higher equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between RSAFS growth rate and 20-day S&P 500 returns is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window, or the average forward return in top decile of RSAFS growth is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate falling below 4.5% indicates a very tight labor market. This constrains corporate margins via wage pressure and triggers Federal Reserve tightening expectations, both of which weigh on stock prices over a short horizon.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE < 4.5%, the average 5-day S&P 500 return is not negative, or the proportion of negative returns does not exceed 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeds the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS), real interest rates are negative. This combination signals that monetary policy is accommodative despite rising prices, creating an overheating risk that historically precedes equity drawdowns.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds FEDFUNDS, the average 20-day forward return is positive, or the strategy yields a Sharpe ratio below 0.2 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: During established monetary easing cycles, declining federal funds rates compress discount rates and support equity valuations. This mechanism is regime-conditional: it only produces positive returns when the Fed is actively cutting rates.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle (FEDFUNDS falling over at least two consecutive FOMC meetings)
failure_condition: During identified easing cycles, the average 60-day S&P 500 return is negative or not statistically significantly above zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) above 0.5% signals expanding manufacturing activity. Strong supply-side growth translates to higher corporate output and profits, lifting equity prices over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: When INDPRO MoM exceeds 0.5%, the proportion of positive 20-day S&P 500 returns is less than 55%, or the average return is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a self-reinforcing cycle of employment and consumption. This interaction signals broad economic health and robust corporate earnings, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: In periods where both conditions hold, the average 20-day S&P 500 return is not positive, or the frequency of positive returns is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) act as a leading indicator for residential construction, durable goods orders, and broader economic activity. Sustained increases in HOUST predict stronger future GDP growth and corporate earnings, supporting equity prices over a multi-quarter horizon.
failure_condition: The 60-day S&P 500 return is not positively correlated with HOUST growth at a statistically significant level (p > 0.05), or the rank IC is below 0.03 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation rises above 3%, markets anticipate tighter monetary policy from the Federal Reserve. The expectation of higher interest rates increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns.
regime_hint: inflation scare or tightening cycle onset
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3%, the average 20-day S&P 500 return is not negative, or the hit rate (fraction of negative returns) is below 55%.
